# AS2 Connector - Full Documentation

# Sending MDNs to Multiple Partners

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following example shows how to send Message Disposition Notifications (MDNs) to multiple
partners. This example contains two flows:

- The first flow configures an AS2 listener endpoint to receive inbound AS2 messages.
- The second flow receives message payloads and AS2 sender and receiver information via HTTP. It
  then uses the **Send with Sync MDN** operation to send outbound AS2 messages to a target AS2
  endpoint.

This example is similar to the
[Receiving AS2 Messages and Sending Back MDNs](as2-connector-example-receive-send) example, except
that for this example:

- The partner information must already exist in the configured keystore.
- You must configure the partner information as follows in the **Partners** tab of the global
  element:
  - Set the **Listener Mode** field to **Shared mode** and specify details for the additional
    partner.
  - Leave the **Partner config** section of this tab blank or the connector will returns errors. The
    following screenshot highlights this section.

    ![Do not configure fields in the highlighted section of the AS2 Connector Listener config screen](_images/as2-connector-example-multi-caveat.png)

## Configuring an AS2 Listener Endpoint to Receive Inbound AS2 Messages

This flow listens for inbound AS2 messages from AS2 Connector send operations and other sources,
such as mendelson AS2 software and HTTP requests:

![Flow for exposing the AS2 listener endpoint](_images/as2-connector-example-mdn1.png)

You can specify partner information in **shared mode** either by entering it directly or by using an
inline expression.

Paste this code into your Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app. In this XML code, the partner information is entered directly:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:as2-mule4="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4/current/mule-as2-mule4.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Server_Config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<as2-mule4:listener-config name="AS2_Server_Listener" doc:name="AS2 Connector Listener config" httpListenerConfig="HTTP_Server_Config" securityLevel="SIGNED_ENCRYPTED">
		<as2-mule4:self-config as2Name="partnera" x509Alias="partnera" email="support@partnera.com"/>
		<as2-mule4:listener-mode >
			<as2-mule4:shared-mode>
				<as2-mule4:partner-configs >
					<as2-mule4:partner-detail-extended as2Name="partnerb" x509Alias="partnerb" email="support@partnerb.com" />
					<as2-mule4:partner-detail-extended as2Name="partnerc" x509Alias="partnerc" email="support@partnerc.com" />
				</as2-mule4:partner-configs>
			</as2-mule4:shared-mode>
		</as2-mule4:listener-mode>
		<as2-mule4:key-store-config keystorePassword="test" keystorePath="as2/partnera.p12" privateKeyPassword="test" />
	</as2-mule4:listener-config>
	<flow name="AS2Listener" >
		<as2-mule4:as2-listener doc:name="As 2 listener" config-ref="AS2_Server_Listener" path="/as2-receive"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Message Received!" message="Message Received: #[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

To specify partner information with an inline expression, use this XML code:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:as2-mule4="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4/current/mule-as2-mule4.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Server_Config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<as2-mule4:listener-config name="AS2_Server_Listener" doc:name="AS2 Connector Listener config" httpListenerConfig="HTTP_Server_Config" securityLevel="SIGNED_ENCRYPTED">
    	<as2-mule4:self-config as2Name="partnera" x509Alias="partnera" email="support@partnera.com"/>
    	<as2-mule4:listener-mode >
        	<as2-mule4:shared-mode partnerConfigs='#[[&#10;	{as2Name:"partnerb", x509Alias:"partnerb", email:"support@partnerb.com"},&#10;	{as2Name:"partnerc", x509Alias:"partnerc", email:"support@partnerc.com"}&#10;]]' />
    	</as2-mule4:listener-mode>
    	<as2-mule4:key-store-config keystorePassword="test" keystorePath="as2/partnera.p12" privateKeyPassword="test" />
	</as2-mule4:listener-config>
	<flow name="AS2Listener" >
		<as2-mule4:as2-listener doc:name="As 2 listener" config-ref="AS2_Server_Listener" path="/as2-receive"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Message Received!" message="Message Received: #[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Sending Outbound AS2 Messages to the Configured AS2 Endpoint

This flow listens for messages, logs a message and its payload, and then uses the **Send with Sync
MDN** operation to forward the message to the exposed AS2 endpoint:

![Flow for forwarding AS2 messages to the exposed endpoint](_images/as2-connector-mdn2-example.png)

Paste this code into your Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:tls="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:as2-mule4="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4/current/mule-as2-mule4.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls/current/mule-tls.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" basePath="b2b" >
		<http:listener-connection protocol="HTTPS" host="0.0.0.0" port="${https.port}" >
			<tls:context >
				<tls:key-store type="jks" path="rootca.jks" alias="rootca" keyPassword="test" password="test" />
			</tls:context>
		</http:listener-connection>
	</http:listener-config>
	<configuration-properties doc:name="Configuration properties" file="app.properties" />
	<as2-mule4:send-config name="AS2_Connector_Send_config" doc:name="AS2 Connector Send config" >
		<as2-mule4:connection partnerURL="#[vars.as2URL]" >
			<tls:context >
				<tls:trust-store insecure="true" />
			</tls:context>
		</as2-mule4:connection>
		<as2-mule4:self-config as2Name="#[vars.as2Sender]" x509Alias="#[vars.as2Sender]" email="support@forward-van.com" />
		<as2-mule4:partner-config as2Name="#[vars.as2Receiver]" x509Alias="#[vars.as2Receiver]" email="support@mythical.com"/>
		<as2-mule4:requester-config subject="EDI" messageIntegrityCheckAlgorithm="SHA256" mdnMessageIntegrityCheckAlgorithm="SHA256" encryptionAlgorithm="DES_EDE3" requestReceipt="SIGNED_REQUIRED"/>
		<as2-mule4:keystore-config keystorePassword="test" keystorePath="as2/forward-van.p12" privateKeyPassword="test" />
	</as2-mule4:send-config>
	<flow name="forward-van-as2-simulatorFlow1" >
		<http:listener doc:name="/forward-van/as2-send" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/forward-van/as2-send"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message="Forward VAN is sending IDOC to Mythical via AS2"/>
		<set-variable value="#[attributes.headers.'as2-URL']" doc:name="as2URL" variableName="as2URL"/>
		<set-variable value="#[attributes.headers.'as2-from']" doc:name="as2Sender" variableName="as2Sender"/>
		<set-variable value="#[attributes.headers.'as2-to']" doc:name="as2Receiver" variableName="as2Receiver"/>
		<as2-mule4:send-with-sync-mdn doc:name="Send with Sync MDN" config-ref="AS2_Connector_Send_config" outputMimeType="application/EDI-X12">
			<as2-mule4:custom-headers ><![CDATA[#[output application/java
---
{
	"PLANT-CODE" : "ABC-12345"
}]]]></as2-mule4:custom-headers>
		</as2-mule4:send-with-sync-mdn>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message="#[output application/json --- attributes]"/>
		<set-payload value="#[output application/json --- attributes.as2MdnAttributes]" doc:name="Set Payload" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [AS2 Connector Examples](as2-connector-examples)
- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Receiving AS2 Messages and Sending Back MDNs

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

These examples show how to use Anypoint Connector for AS2 (AS2 Connector) to receive AS2 messages
from a trading partner and send MDNs (Message Disposition Notifications) to that partner in
response:

- [Receiving AS2 Messages](#receiving-messages)

  Receive AS2 messages from your partner and, for every received message, log a message that
  contains the message payload to the Studio console.

- [Sending Synchronous AS2 MDNs](#synchronous-mdns)

  Send a synchronous AS2 MDN in response to each received AS2 message.

- [Sending Asynchronous AS2 MDNs](#asynchronous-mdns)

  Send an asynchronous AS2 MDN in response to each received AS2 message.

## Receiving AS2 Messages

Creating this example involves creating a new Mule project and configuring the **As 2 Listener**
source and **Logger** component.

The following screenshot shows the Anypoint Studio app flow for this example:

![Receive AS2 Messages App Flow](_images/as2-connector-example-listener-flow.png)

### Create a Flow to Receive the Message

Configure the **AS 2 Listener** source to initiate a Mule flow when a call is made to the
`/as2-receive` path on local host port `8081`:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a new Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select the **As 2 listener** source and drag it to the canvas.
3.  On the **As 2 listener** properties screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Set the **Path** field to `/as2-receive`.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the **As 2 listener** source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **HTTP Listener** field.
7.  Accept the defaults and click **OK**.
8.  On the **General** tab, configure the keystore that contains the certificates and keys:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Keystore Password</p></td><td><p><code>test</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keystore Path</p></td><td><p><code>as2/partnera.p12</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private Key Password</p></td><td><p><code>test</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

9.  On the **Partners** tab, specify the self and partner credentials.

    For the self credentials, configure these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>AS2 Partner Name</p></td><td><p><code>partnera</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>x509 Alias</p></td><td><p><code>partnera</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>email</p></td><td><p><code>support@partnera.com</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    For the partner credentials, configure these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>AS2 Partner Name</p></td><td><p><code>partnerb</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>x509 Alias</p></td><td><p><code>partnerb</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>email</p></td><td><p><code>support@partnerb.com</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Add the Logger Component

Add the **Logger** component to the flow and configure it to log a message that includes the payload
of the received message:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core** and drag the **Logger** component next to **As 2
    listener**.
2.  In the Logger properties screen, set the **Message** field to `Message Received: #[payload]`.

### Save and Test the Mule App

1.  Save the project.
2.  Run the app by clicking the project name in **Package Explorer** and then clicking **Run** >
    **Run As** > **Mule Application**.

    If the application starts correctly, the console displays this message:

    `Mule is up and kicking`

3.  Test the app by opening `http://localhost:8081/as2-receive` from a browser or an app like
    Postman and adding the required credentials.

    For each new request, the Studio console displays the received message. For example:

```console
INFO  2022-09-09 17:15:46,409 [[MuleRuntime].uber.06: [as2-basic-example].AS2Listener.CPU_LITE @1369de96] [processor:
AS2Listener/processors/0; event: 30609f70-307c-11ed-8f5a-38f9d3713331] org.mule.runtime.core.internal.processor.
LoggerMessageProcessor: Message Received: Test Message
```

### XML for the Receiving AS2 Messages Example

Paste this code into your Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:as2-mule4="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4/current/mule-as2-mule4.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Server_Config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<as2-mule4:listener-config name="AS2_Server_Listener" doc:name="AS2 Connector Listener config" httpListenerConfig="HTTP_Server_Config" securityLevel="SIGNED_ENCRYPTED">
		<as2-mule4:self-config as2Name="partnera" x509Alias="partnera" email="support@partnera.com"/>
		<as2-mule4:partner-config as2Name="partnerb" x509Alias="partnerb" email="support@partnerb.com" />
		<as2-mule4:key-store-config keystorePassword="test" keystorePath="as2/partnera.p12" privateKeyPassword="test" />
	</as2-mule4:listener-config>
	<flow name="AS2Listener" >
		<as2-mule4:as2-listener doc:name="As 2 listener" config-ref="AS2_Server_Listener" path="/as2-receive"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Message Received!" message="Message Received: #[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Sending Synchronous AS2 MDNs

When your trading partner uses an AS2 listener, like in the
[Receiving AS2 Messages](#receiving-messages-example) example, the listenerâs MDN mode controls when
a synchronous MDN is returned. The AS2 listener supports these configurations for managing
synchronous MDN delivery timing:

- **Immediate**

  The listener returns the MDN as soon as the incoming message is parsed and validated. The
  receiverâs business flow continues as a separate process after the MDN is sent. This is the
  default MDN Mode.

- **Auto**

  The listener returns the MDN only after its internal business flow completes. This ensures that if
  the flow fails, a negative MDN is returned to the sender to signal a processing error.

  For this configuration, set the MDN mode to `AUTO` on the AS2 listener.

      <as2-mule4:as2-listener doc:name="As 2 listener" config-ref="AS2\_Server\_Listener" path="/as2-receive" mdnMode="AUTO"/>

## Sending Asynchronous AS2 MDNs

The AS2 listener validates the presence of the `Disposition-Notification-To` and
`Receipt-Delivery-Option` headers when receiving an asynchronous MDN request. The listener
immediately returns an `HTTP 200 OK` to acknowledge the inbound message. The listener uses the URL
in the `Receipt-Delivery-Option` header to return the MDN via a new HTTP connection.

The AS2 listener supports these configurations for managing asynchronous MDN delivery timing:

- **Immediate**

  The listener posts the MDN to the `Receipt-Delivery-Option` URL as soon as the inbound message is
  parsed and validated. This is the default MDN Mode.

- **Auto**

  The listener posts the MDN to the `Receipt-Delivery-Option` URL only after its business flow
  completes, allowing a negative MDN to be sent if the flow fails.

  For this configuration, set the MDN mode to `AUTO` on the AS2 listener.

      <as2-mule4:as2-listener doc:name="As 2 listener" config-ref="AS2\_Server\_Listener" path="/as2-receive" mdnMode="AUTO"/>

## See Also

- [AS2 Connector Examples](as2-connector-examples)
- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Receiving AS2 MDNs

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following example shows how to use Anypoint Connector for AS2 (AS2 Connector) to receive AS2 MDN
(Message Disposition Notification) messages from a trading partner and, for every received MDN, log
a message that contains the message payload to the Studio console.

This example is similar to the
[Receiving AS2 Messages](as2-connector-example-receive-send#receiving-messages) example, except that
in this example:

- You use the **AS2 mdn listener** source instead of the **As 2 listener** source and set the path
  to `/receiveMDN`.
- On the **Logger** properties window, you set the **Message** field to `MDN Received: #[payload]`.

The following screenshot shows the Anypoint Studio app flow for this example:

![Receive MDN Messages Example App Flow](_images/as2-connector-example-mdn-listener-flow.png)

To test this example:

1.  Run this app.
2.  Run the app configured in the
    [Sending Asynchronous AS2 MDNs](as2-connector-example-receive-send#asynchronous-mdns) example.

    In the Studio console in this app, you should see a message for each received AS2 MDN that was
    sent asynchronously. For example:

    ```console
    INFO  2022-09-09 17:15:46,409 [[MuleRuntime].uber.06: [as2-basic-example].ReceiveAsyncMDN.CPU_LITE @1369de96] [processor:
    ReceiveAsyncMDN/processors/0; event: 30609f70-307c-11ed-8f5a-38f9d3713331] org.mule.runtime.core.internal.processor.
    LoggerMessageProcessor: MDN Received!
    ```

> [!NOTE] If the **Receipt delivery url** field is not configured for the **Send with Async MDN**
> source and you are using an HTTP request, the header `Receipt-Delivery-Option` must be included
> with the **As 2 mdn listener** endpoint (`http://localhost:8082/receiveMDN`).

## XML for the Receiving AS2 MDNs Example

Paste this code into your Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:as2-mule4="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4/current/mule-as2-mule4.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="localhost" port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<as2-mule4:mdn-listener-config name="AS2_Connector_Mdn_listener_config" doc:name="AS2 Connector Mdn listener config" httpListenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config" >
		<as2-mule4:self-config as2Name="partnera" x509Alias="partnera" email="support@partnera.com" />
		<as2-mule4:partner-config as2Name="partnerb" x509Alias="partnerb" email="support@partnerb.com" />
		<as2-mule4:key-store-config keystorePassword="test" keystorePath="as2/partnera.p12" privateKeyPassword="test" />
	</as2-mule4:mdn-listener-config>
	<flow name="AS2MDNListener" >
		<as2-mule4:as2-mdn-listener doc:name="As 2 mdn listener"path="/receiveMDN" config-ref="AS2_Connector_Mdn_listener_config"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Message Received!" message="MDN Received: #[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [AS2 Connector Examples](as2-connector-examples)
- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Sending AS2 Messages and Receiving MDNs

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

These examples show how to use Anypoint Connector for AS2 (AS2 Connector) to send AS2 messages to a
trading partner and receive Message Disposition Notifications (MDNs) in response:

- [Send with Synchronous MDN](#send-with-synchronous-mdn)

  Send an AS2 message and receive a synchronous MDN in response.

- [Send with Asynchronous MDN](#send-with-asynchronous-mdn)

  Send an AS2 message and receive an asynchronous MDN in response.

## Send with Synchronous MDN

To create this example, start a new Mule project and configure the `http:listener` source and the
Logger component.

The image shows the Anypoint Studio flow for this example:

![Anypoint Studio flow for sending AS2 messages and receiving synchronous MDNs](_images/as2-connector-example-sync-flow.png)

### Create a Flow to Send an AS2 Message

Follow these steps to configure the `http:listener` source to initiate a Mule flow when a call is
made to the `/sendSyncFlow` path on localhost port 8082:

1.  Drag the HTTP listener from the Mule Palette onto the canvas.
2.  Optionally, change the display name on the properties window for the listener and click **OK**.
3.  Set the **Path** field to `/sendSyncFlow`.
4.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP listener.
5.  On the **General** tab, accept the defaults and click **OK**.

### Add the Send with Synchronous MDN Operation

Follow these steps to add the **Send with Sync MDN** operation and configure it to send MDNs to the
receiving endpoint of `partnera`:

1.  Drag the **Send with Sync MDN** operation next to the listener component on the canvas.
2.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element for the **Send with Sync MDN** operation.
3.  Configure the connection by setting the **Partner URL** field to
    `http://localhost:8081/as2-receive` on the **General** tab.
4.  Specify the self and partner credentials.

    Configure these fields in the **Self Config** section:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>AS2 Partner Name</p></td><td><p>partnerb</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>x509 Alias</p></td><td><p>partnerb</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>email</p></td><td><p><a href="mailto:support@partnerb.com">support@partnerb.com</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    Configure these fields in the **Partner Config** section:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>AS2 Partner Name</p></td><td><p>partnera</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>x509 Alias</p></td><td><p>partnera</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>email</p></td><td><p><a href="mailto:support@partnera.com">support@partnera.com</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  If you want to use algorithms, configure them in the **Requester Config** section.
6.  In the **Keystore** section, apply these values to the keystore that contains the certificates
    and keys:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Keystore Password</p></td><td><p>test</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keystore Path</p></td><td><p>as2/partnerb.p12</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private Key Password</p></td><td><p>test</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Add the Logger Component

Add the logger component and configure it to log a message when the app sends an AS2 message and
receives a synchronous MDN.

1.  From the Mule Palette, select **Core** and drag the logger component next to the **Send with
    Sync MDN** operation.
2.  Set the message for the logger to `Message Sent and MDN Received!`.

### Save and Test the Mule App

Follow these steps to deploy your application locally and verify that it correctly sends AS2
requests and receives synchronous MDNs.

1.  Save the project.
2.  Run the app by clicking the project name in **Package Explorer** and then clicking **Run** >
    **Run As** > **Mule Application**.

    If the application starts correctly, the console shows a success message.

3.  Test the app by opening `http://localhost:8082/sendSyncFlow` from a browser or an app like
    Postman.

You can now send AS2 requests to the endpoint configured in the
[Receiving AS2 Messages and Sending Back MDNs](as2-connector-example-receive-send#receiving-messages-example)
example. With the example running, you can observe the message sent by the **Send with Sync MDN**
operation.

### XML for the Sending Synchronous AS2 MDNs Example

Paste this code into your Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:as2-mule4="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4/current/mule-as2-mule4.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Client_Config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<as2-mule4:send-config name="AS2_Client_Send_Sync" doc:name="AS2 Connector Send config" >
		<as2-mule4:connection partnerURL="http://localhost:8081/as2-receive" />
		<as2-mule4:self-config as2Name="partnerb" x509Alias="partnerb" email="support@partnerb.com" />
		<as2-mule4:partner-config as2Name="partnera" x509Alias="partnera" email="support@partnera.com" />
		<as2-mule4:requester-config messageIntegrityCheckAlgorithm="SHA512" mdnMessageIntegrityCheckAlgorithm="SHA512" encryptionAlgorithm="DES_EDE3" requestReceipt="SIGNED_REQUIRED" />
		<as2-mule4:keystore-config keystorePassword="test" keystorePath="as2/partnerb.p12" privateKeyPassword="test" />
	</as2-mule4:send-config>
	<flow name="SendSyncFlow" >
		<http:listener doc:name="/sendSyncFlow" config-ref="HTTP_Client_Config" path="/sendSyncFlow" />
		<as2-mule4:send-with-sync-mdn doc:name="Send with Sync MDN" config-ref="AS2_Client_Send_Sync"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Message Sent and MDN Received!" message="Message Sent and MDN Received!" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Send with Async MDN

This example follows the same structure as the [Send with Sync MDN](#send-with-synchronous-mdn)
example but incorporates these changes:

- The **Send with Async MDN** operation is used instead of the **Send with Sync MDN** operation.
- A value is specified for the **Receipt delivery url** field. The field can be configured in the
  global element for the **Send with Async MDN** operation or in the properties window.

The image shows the Anypoint Studio flow for this example:

![Anypoint Studio flow for sending AS2 messages and receiving asynchronous MDNs](_images/as2-connector-example-async-flow.png)

### XML for the Sending AS2 Asynchronous MDNs Example

Paste this code into your Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:as2-mule4="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4/current/mule-as2-mule4.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Client_Config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<as2-mule4:send-config name="AS2_Client_Send_Sync" doc:name="AS2 Connector Send config" >
		<as2-mule4:connection partnerURL="http://localhost:8081/as2-receive" />
		<as2-mule4:self-config as2Name="partnerb" x509Alias="partnerb" email="support@partnerb.com" />
		<as2-mule4:partner-config as2Name="partnera" x509Alias="partnera" email="support@partnera.com" />
		<as2-mule4:requester-config messageIntegrityCheckAlgorithm="SHA512" mdnMessageIntegrityCheckAlgorithm="SHA512" encryptionAlgorithm="DES_EDE3" requestReceipt="SIGNED_REQUIRED" />
		<as2-mule4:keystore-config keystorePassword="test" keystorePath="as2/partnerb.p12" privateKeyPassword="test" />
	</as2-mule4:send-config>
	<flow name="SendSyncFlow" >
		<http:listener doc:name="/sendAsyncFlow" config-ref="HTTP_Client_Config" path="/sendAsyncFlow" />
		<as2-mule4:send-with-async-mdn doc:name="Send with Async MDN" config-ref="AS2_Client_Send_Sync" receiptDeliveryUrl="http://localhost:8082/receiveMDN"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Message Sent!" message="Message Sent!" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Save and Test the Mule App

Follow these steps to deploy your application locally and verify that it correctly sends AS2
requests and receives asynchronous MDNs.

1.  Save the project.
2.  Run the app by clicking the project name in **Package Explorer** and then clicking **Run** >
    **Run As** > **Mule Application**.

    If the application starts correctly, the console shows a success message.

3.  Test the app by opening `http://localhost:8082/sendAsyncFlow` from a browser or an app like
    Postman.

You can now send AS2 requests to the endpoint configured in the
[Receiving AS2 Messages and Sending Back MDNs](as2-connector-example-receive-send#receiving-messages-example)
example. With the example running, you can observe the message sent by the **Send with Async MDN**
operation.

The MDN is omitted from the logs because itâs routed to the `receiptDeliveryUrl` shown in the
[Receiving AS2 MDNs](as2-connector-example-receiving-mdn-messages) example.

## See Also

- [AS2 Connector Examples](as2-connector-examples)
- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# AS2 Connector 7.0 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Mule applications using Anypoint Connector for AS2 (AS2 Connector) must include a global
configuration to add external libraries specific to the active security mode. Only one external libs
configuration is permitted per application, so you must configure this element manually in the
examples.

These examples show how to configure send and receive messages in AS2 Connector:

- [Receiving AS2 Messages and Sending Back MDNs](as2-connector-example-receive-send)  
  Configure the **As 2 listener** source to start receiving AS2 requests and the **Send with Sync
  MDN** and **Send with Async MDN** operations to start sending AS2 requests.
- [Receiving AS2 MDN Messages](as2-connector-example-receiving-mdn-messages)  
  Configure the **As 2 mdn listener** source to start receiving Messsage Disposition Notification
  (MDN) Messages.
- [Sending AS2 Messages and Receiving MDNs](as2-connector-example-send)  
  Configure the connector to send AS2 messages to a partner and handle both synchronous and
  asynchronous MDNs.
- [Sending MDNs to Multiple Partners](as2-connector-example-multiple-partners)  
  How to send MDNs to multiple partners.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# AS2 Connector 7.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for AS2 (AS2 Connector) enables trading partners to transmit and receive AS2
messages that contain EDI-X12, EDIFACT, XML, or binary payloads.

## Configurations

---

### Listener Config

#### General Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ListenerConfig_async-mdn-connection-provider">Async Mdn Connection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>HTTP Listener</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reference to global HTTP Listener definition for our service endpoint used by partner for both action and signal messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security Requirement Level</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>SIGNED</p></li><li><p>ENCRYPTED</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_ENCRYPTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The security level required for any AS2 Listener using this configuration for all incoming messages. An error MDN response results if the security requirement is not met.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keystore Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password of the provided keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keystore Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Absolute or relative path to the keystore containing certificates and private keys for self (this application) and the partner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Private key password, if the private key to use for signing the MDN is password protected.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Validate certificate expiration date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If enabled, the connector canât receive or send messages if the certificate is expired.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Warning before (days)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of days before the certificate expiration date when the connector sends a warning message that the certificate is about to expire. For example, if the value of this field is <code>3</code>, then the connector sends a warning message three days before the certificate expiration date.<br><br>This field applies only when the value of this field is greater than <code>0</code> and the <strong>Validate certificate expiration date</strong> field is enabled.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Partners Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Inbound Request Authentication</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2BasicAuthentication">AS2 Basic Authentication</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Authentication method to use for verifying the incoming HTTP request when using dedicated mode.</p></div><div><p>Do not select this field if you are using shared mode or you will receive an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - AS2 Partner Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 system identifier used for AS2-From and AS2-To headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - x509 Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate, private key alias, or both in the keystore. Required if using signing or encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - email</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address used as the HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending AS2 messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use different Alias for MDN Signature</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Applies to the <strong>AS2 Listener</strong> source) Use the alias specified in <strong>Alias For MDN Signature</strong> to use a different keystore entry for the MDN signature.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias For MDN Signature</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Applies to the <strong>AS2 Listener</strong> source) Alias to use for the MDN signature if using a different alias for the MDN signature than for decryption. This field takes effect when <strong>Use different Alias for MDN Signature</strong> is <code>true</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Private key password, if the private key to use for signing the MDN is password protected.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - Listener Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Dedicated mode</p></li><li><p>Shared mode</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Dedicated mode</p><div><p>Enables you to configure a single AS2 listener endpoint to receive messages from one partner only.</p></div><div><p>If you select <strong>Dedicated mode</strong>, check <strong>Partner config</strong> (mandatory) and configure the partner using the Dedicated mode fields in this table.</p></div></li><li><p>Shared Mode</p><div><p>Enables you to configure a single AS2 listener endpoint to receive messages from multiple partners.</p></div><div><p>If you select <strong>Shared mode</strong>, uncheck <strong>Partner config</strong> (mandatory) and configure each partner using the Shared mode fields in this table.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Dedicated mode</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Dedicated mode - Partner Config - AS2 Partner Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 system identifier used for AS2-From and AS2-To headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Dedicated mode - Partner Config - x509 Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate, private key alias, or both in the keystore. Required if using signing or encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Dedicated mode - Partner Config - email</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address used as the HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending AS2 messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Shared mode - Partners</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Expression</p></li><li><p>Inline</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to add partner information by using an expression or by entering values inline.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Shared mode - Partners - AS2 Partner Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 system identifier used for AS2-From and AS2-To headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Shared mode - Partners - x509 Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate, private key alias, or both in the keystore. Required if using signing or encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Shared mode - Partners - email</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address used as the HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending an AS2 message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Shared mode - Inbound Request Authentication</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2BasicAuthentication">AS2 Basic Authentication</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Authentication method to use for verifying the incoming HTTP request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Advanced Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Check for duplicate messages</strong></p></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checks for duplicate messages:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>If selected, the connector checks to see if a new received message is duplicated, as determined by comparing the following values in the new message to those in existing messages:</p><div><ul><li><p>Sender (from the AS2-From field)</p></li><li><p>Receiver (from the AS2-To field)</p></li><li><p>Message ID (from the Message-Id field)</p></li></ul></div><div><p>If all three values match an existing message, the connector rejects the duplicate message. If selected, you must:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Reference a global object store in the <strong>Object Store</strong> field. This object store will store the existing messages.</p></li><li><p>Add the object store dependency to your project by adding the <strong>ObjectStore</strong> component to the Studio canvas or adding it to the <code>pom.xml</code>, as follows:</p><div><div><pre>&lt;dependency&gt;
  &lt;groupId&gt;org.mule.connectors&lt;/groupId&gt;
  &lt;artifactId&gt;mule-objectstore-connector&lt;/artifactId&gt;
  &lt;version&gt;_latest-version_&lt;/version&gt;
  &lt;classifier&gt;mule-plugin&lt;/classifier&gt;
&lt;/dependency&gt;</pre></div></div><div><p>For the latest object store version, see the <a href="../../release-notes/connector/object-store-connector-release-notes-mule-4">Object Store Connector Release Notes</a>.<br><br>If the MDN mode is <code>AUTO</code>, use the Idempotent Message Validator instead of the <strong>Check for duplicate messages</strong> field to check for duplicate messages. If the MDN mode is <code>IMMEDIATE</code>, the validator does not work. Therefore, in this circumstance, you must use the <strong>Check for duplicate messages</strong> field to check for duplicate messages. For more information, see <a href="../../mule-runtime/latest/idempotent-message-validator">Idempotent Message Validator</a>.<br><br></p></div></li><li><p>If deselected, the connector accepts duplicate messages.</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object store where the connector stores new messages. This field is mandatory if the <strong>Check for duplicate messages</strong> field is selected.<br><br>For information about setting field values for this object store, see the <a href="../../object-store-connector/latest/object-store-connector-reference">Object Store Connector Reference</a>.<br><br>You might experience limitations that are specific to the object store implementation you are using (Anypoint Object Store V2 (OSv2), CloudHub Object Store V1 (OSv1), or Mule Object Store), so configure the object store to suit your needs. For more information, see <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=001123185&amp;type=1">The Different Types of Object Stores Explained.</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Async MDN Connection Provider

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Persistent Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, each connection is closed after the first request completes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. This value is qualified by the <strong>Connection Timeout Units</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Units</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the value of the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of outbound connections to keep open at the same time. By default, the number of connections is unlimited.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed. The value of this attribute is only used when persistent connections are enabled. This value is qualified by the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout Units</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout Units</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the value of the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MINUTES</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Retry Interval</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The interval between each retry, in seconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>60</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Retry Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of retries. A value of <code>0</code> means no retries.)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ntlm-proxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy. A proxy element must define a host name and a port attributes, and optionally can define a username and password.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reference to a TLS config element. This enables HTTPS for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, the deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Sources

- [As2 Listener](#as2-listener)

---

### MDN Listener Config

#### General Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>HTTP Listener</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reference to global HTTP Listener definition for our service endpoint used by a partner for both action and signal messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keystore Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password of the provided keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keystore Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Absolute or relative path to the keystore containing certificates and private keys for self(this application) and the partner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Private key password, if the private key to use for signing the MDN is password protected.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Validate certificate expiration date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If enabled, the connector canât receive or send messages if the certificate is expired.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Warning before (days)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of days before the certificate expiration date when the connector sends a warning message that the certificate is about to expire. For example, if the value of this field is <code>3</code>, then the connector sends a warning message three days before the certificate expiration date.<br><br>This field applies only when the value of this field is greater than <code>0</code> and the <strong>Validate certificate expiration date</strong> field is enabled.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Advanced Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Partners Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - AS2 Partner Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 system identifier used for AS2-From and AS2-To headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - x509 Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate, private key alias, or both in the keystore. Required if using signing or encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - email</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address used as the HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending 12 messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MDN Listener Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Mdn dedicated mode</p></li><li><p>Mdn shared mode</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Mdn dedicated mode</p><div><p>Enables you to configure a single AS2 listener endpoint to receive MDNs from one partner only.</p></div><div><p>If you select <strong>Mdn dedicated mode</strong>, check <strong>Partner config</strong> (mandatory) and configure the partner using the Mdn dedicated mode fields in this table.</p></div></li><li><p>Mdn shared mode</p><div><p>Enables you to configure a single AS2 listener endpoint to receive MDNs from multiple partners.</p></div><div><p>If you select <strong>Mdn shared mode</strong>, uncheck <strong>Partner config</strong> (mandatory) and configure each partner using the Mdn shared mode fields in this table.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn dedicated mode - Partner Config - AS2 Partner Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 system identifier used for AS2-From and AS2-To headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn dedicated mode - Partner Config - x509 Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate, private key alias, or both in the keystore. Required if using signing or encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Dedicated mode</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn shared mode - Partners</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Expression</p></li><li><p>Inline</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to add partner information by using an expression or by entering values inline.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn dedicated mode - Partner Config - email</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address used as the HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending MDNs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn shared mode - Partners - AS2 Partner Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 system identifier used for AS2-From and AS2-To headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn shared mode - Partners - x509 Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate, private key alias, or both in the keystore. Required if using signing or encryption.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn shared mode - Partners - email</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address used as the HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending MDNs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Sources

- [As2 Mdn Listener](#as2-mdn-listener)

---

### Send Config

This class represents an extension configuration, values set in this class are commonly used across
multiple operations since they represent something core from the extension.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#send-config_connection">Outbound Connection Config</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>General - Transfer Mode Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Chunked transfer mode</p></li><li><p>Identity transfer mode</p></li><li><p>Threshold transfer mode</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The transfer mode applied when sending information:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Chunked transfer mode</p><div><p>Sends the headers without the Content-Length property and modifies the message body to transfer it as a series of chunks.</p></div></li><li><p>Identity transfer mode</p><div><p>Sends the headers with the Content-Length property and sends the whole message body.</p></div></li><li><p>Threshold transfer mode</p><div><p>Sends the headers and message body according to the specified threshold.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>If the message body is less than or equal to the threshold, the connector uses the Identity transfer mode option.</p></li><li><p>If the message body is greater than the threshold, the connector uses the Chunked transfer mode option.</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Chunked transfer mode</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Threshold</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Threshold used when the General - Transfer Mode Configuration field is set to <code>Threshold transfer mode</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>64</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Threshold Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Byte</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit associated with the Threshold field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>KB</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>General - Propagate Content Transfer Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to propagate the content transfer encoding on the AS2 message sent to the partner:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>True: The main content transfer encoding is propagated to every MIME part of the resulting message.</p></li><li><p>False: Signed and encrypted MIME parts are encoded with Base64.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>False</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>General - Custom Message ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the specification of a custom AS2 Message ID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partner Config - AS2 Partner Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 system identifier (used for AS2-From and AS2-To headers)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partner Config - x509 Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate, private key alias, or both in the keystore (required if using signing or encryption)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partner Config - email</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address used as HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MDN Partner Config - Use different Alias for MDN Signature</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Applies to the <strong>Send with Sync Mdn</strong> operation) Use the alias specified in <strong>Alias For MDN Signature</strong> as the alias for MDN signature validation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MDN Partner Config - Alias For MDN Signature</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Applies to the <strong>Send with Sync Mdn</strong> operation) Alias to use for MDN signature validation if using a different alias for MDN signature validation than for encryption. This field takes effect when <strong>Use different Alias for MDN Signature</strong> is <code>true</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - AS2 Partner Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 system identifier (used for AS2-From and AS2-To headers)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - x509 Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate, private key alias or both in keystore (required if using signing or encryption)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Self config - email</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email address used as HTTP <code>From</code> header when sending</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Free form text for MIME Subject header</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MIC Signature Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MD5</p></li><li><p>SHA1</p></li><li><p>SHA224</p></li><li><p>SHA256</p></li><li><p>SHA384</p></li><li><p>SHA512</p></li><li><p>UNSIGNED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Algorithm to use when setting the message integrity check value.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>UNSIGNED</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>MDN MIC Signature Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MD5</p></li><li><p>SHA1</p></li><li><p>SHA224</p></li><li><p>SHA256</p></li><li><p>SHA384</p></li><li><p>SHA512</p></li><li><p>UNSIGNED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Request partner to respond with a signed MDN using this algorithm for calculating MIC</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>UNSIGNED</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encryption Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DES</p></li><li><p>DES_EDE3</p></li><li><p>RC2</p></li><li><p>AES128_CBC</p></li><li><p>AES192_CBC</p></li><li><p>AES256_CBC</p></li><li><p>AES128_CCM</p></li><li><p>AES192_CCM</p></li><li><p>AES256_CCM</p></li><li><p>AES128_GCM</p></li><li><p>AES192_GCM</p></li><li><p>AES256_GCM</p></li><li><p>AES256_WRAP</p></li><li><p>CAST5</p></li><li><p>UNENCRYPTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Algorithm to use when encrypting the message to be sent</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>UNENCRYPTED</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Transfer Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BASE64</p></li><li><p>QUOTED_PRINTABLE</p></li><li><p>SEVEN_BIT</p></li><li><p>EIGHT_BIT</p></li><li><p>BINARY</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding to use on the content of AS2 message being sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>BINARY</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request A Receipt</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>UNSIGNED</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_OPTIONAL</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_REQUIRED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector behaves based on the received receipts.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>UNSIGNED</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Compression Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>ZLIB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Compression type to use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receipt Delivery URL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The asynchronous MDN delivery address to which the MDN is returned. This should be the fully formed URL to the <strong>As 2 mdn listener</strong> source, for example, <code>https://example.com/mdn</code>.<br><br>If a flow uses the <strong>Send With Async MDN</strong> operation, then either this field or the <strong>Receipt Delivery URL</strong> field associated with that operation is required. If both fields have values, the value for the operation overrides the value for the global configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keystore Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password of the provided keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keystore Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Absolute or relative path to the keystore containing certificates and private keys for self(this application) and the partner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Private key password, if the private key to use for signing the MDN is password protected.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Validate certificate expiration date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If enabled, the connector canât receive or send messages if the certificate is expired.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Warning before (days)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of days before the certificate expiration date when the connector sends a warning message that the certificate is about to expire. For example, if the value of this field is <code>3</code>, then the connector sends a warning message three days before the certificate expiration date.<br><br>This field applies only when the value of this field is greater than <code>0</code> and the <strong>Validate certificate expiration date</strong> field is enabled.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Outbound Connection Config

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Persistent Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, each connection is closed after the first request completes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. This value is qualified by the <strong>Connection Timeout Units</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Units</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the value of the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of outbound connections to keep open at the same time. By default the number of connections is unlimited.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed. The value of this attribute is only used when persistent connections are enabled. This value is qualified by the value of the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout Units</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout Units</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the value of the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MINUTES</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#proxy">Proxy</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ntlm-proxy">NTLM Proxy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy. A proxy element must define a host name and a port attributes, and optionally can define a username and a password.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partner URL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Service endpoint URL to the partner.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Outbound Request Authentication</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>HttpRequestAuthentication</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Authentication method to use for the HTTP request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reference to a TLS config element. This enables HTTPS for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, the deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Sources

- [As2 Listener](#as2-listener)
- [As2 Mdn Listener](#as2-mdn-listener)
- [Non repudiation listener](#non-repudiation-listener)

### As2 Listener

`<as2-mule4:as2-listener>`

Represents a listener for AS2 sender requests.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This path is appended to any path set in the listener configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>/</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An override for the timeout for establishing connections to the remote service as milliseconds. The default of 0 means this value is not used to override the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non repudiation flow ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configured flow that contains the <strong>Non repudiation listener</strong> source.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MDN deliver mode:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO: Sends an MDN at the end of the flow.</p></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE: Sends an MDN when the source receives a message.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>IMMEDIATE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams.
