The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2ListenerAttributes">AS2 Listener Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Listener Config](#ListenerConfig)

### As2 Mdn Listener

`<as2-mule4:as2-mdn-listener>`

Listens for AS2 requests and automatically sends an MDN back to the receipt URL provided in the
transmission when a request is received.

> [!NOTE] If the receipt URL is inaccessible, the inbound AS2 message is not processed.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This path is appended to any path set in the listener configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>/</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non repudiation flow ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configured flow that contains the <strong>Non repudiation listener</strong> source.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2MdnAttributes">AS2 Mdn Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [MDN Listener Config](#MDNListenerConfig)

### Non repudiation listener

`<as2-mule4:non-repudiation-listener>`

Receives a signed copy of a receipt or a message for persistent non-repudiation storage.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Display Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload passed to this flow.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload passed to this flow.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>Non repeatable stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2ListenerAttributes">AS2 Listener Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2MdnAttributes">AS2 Mdn Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

None.

## Operations

- [Send With Async Mdn](#sendWithAsyncMdn)
- [Send With Sync Mdn](#sendWithSyncMdn)

### Send With Async Mdn

`<as2-mule4:send-with-async-mdn>`

Performs the send async request using the configuration, client, and AS2 requester parameters, and
completes the callback accordingly.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>General - Custom Message ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the specification of a custom AS2 Message ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>AS2 MIME Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content type of the Document being sent via AS2 (for example, <code>application/EDI-X12</code>). There are no restrictions on the content-type that are supported.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload.^mimeType]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Stream</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content InputStream to be sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content MIME part content description</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Filename of the content stream. If not set, the content is transferred without a filename.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Receipt</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>UNSIGNED</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_OPTIONAL</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_REQUIRED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector behaves based on the received receipts:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE: No receipt is expected.</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_OPTIONAL: Receipt can be signed or unsigned.</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_REQUIRED: Expects a signed receipt. The application fails if the receipt is unsigned.</p></li><li><p>UNSIGNED: Expects an unsigned receipt.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Require Processed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Requires a processed response in the MDN to continue the flow. An error occurs if the response is unprocessed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Override for the timeout for establishing connections to the remote service as milliseconds. The default of 0 means this value is not used to override the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non repudiation flow ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configured flow that contains the <strong>Non repudiation listener</strong> source.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables you to add custom keys to include in outbound AS2 messages. For each key to include, enter values for these name-value pairs:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Name</p><div><p>Name of the key to send to outbound AS2 messages, formatted as <code>Name:key-name</code></p></div></li><li><p>Value</p><div><p>Content of the key to send to outbound AS2 messages, formatted as <code>Value:key-value</code></p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receipt Delivery URL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The asynchronous MDN delivery address to which the MDN is returned. This should be the fully formed URL to the <strong>As 2 mdn listener</strong> source, for example, <code>https://example.com/mdn</code>.<br><br>If a flow uses the <strong>Send With Async MDN</strong> operation, then either this field or the <strong>Receipt Delivery URL</strong> field associated with that operation is required. If both fields have values, the value for the operation overrides the value for the global configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2SendAttributes">AS2 Send Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Send Config](#send-config)

#### Throws

- AS2-MULE4:CONFIGURATION
- AS2-MULE4:UNAUTHORIZED
- AS2-MULE4:CONNECTIVITY
- AS2-MULE4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Send With Sync Mdn

`<as2-mule4:send-with-sync-mdn>`

Performs the send sync request using the configuration, client, and AS2 requester parameters, and
completes the callback accordingly.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>General - Custom Message ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the specification of a custom AS2 Message ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>AS2 MIME Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content type of the Document being sent via AS2 (for example, application/EDI-X12). There are no restrictions on the content-type that are supported.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload.^mimeType]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Stream</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content InputStream to be sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Description</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content MIME part content description</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Filename of the content stream. If not set, the content is transferred without a filename.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Receipt</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>UNSIGNED</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_OPTIONAL</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_REQUIRED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector behaves based on the received receipts:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE: No receipt is expected.</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_OPTIONAL: Receipt can be signed or unsigned.</p></li><li><p>SIGNED_REQUIRED: Expects a signed receipt. The application fails if the receipt is unsigned.</p></li><li><p>UNSIGNED: Expects an unsigned receipt.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Require Processed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Requires a processed response in the MDN to continue the flow. An error occurs if the response is unprocessed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Require Processed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Require Processed response in the MDN to continue the flow (otherwise there is an error)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An override for the timeout for establishing connections to the remote service as milliseconds. The default of 0 means this value is not used to override the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non repudiation flow ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configured flow that contains the <strong>Non repudiation listener</strong> source.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables you to add custom keys to include in outbound AS2 messages. For each key to include, enter values for these name-value pairs:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Name</p><div><p>Name of the key to send to outbound AS2 messages, formatted as <code>Name:key-name</code></p></div></li><li><p>Value</p><div><p>Content of the key to send to outbound AS2 messages, formatted as <code>Value:key-value</code></p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2SendAttributes">AS2 Send Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Send Config](#send-config)

#### Throws

- AS2-MULE4:CONFIGURATION
- AS2-MULE4:MIME_PARSE
- AS2-MULE4:UNAUTHORIZED
- AS2-MULE4:CONNECTIVITY
- AS2-MULE4:SIGNATURE_VERIFY
- AS2-MULE4:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Types

### AS2 Basic Authentication

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to authenticate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password of user to authenticate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### AS2 Listener Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>As2 Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 unique message ID from receiver to sender.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>filename</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Received filename.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type, for example, <code>application/xml</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 Receiver name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 HTTP headers that elaborate the message format.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn Delivery Status</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>SUCCESS</p></li><li><p>FAILURE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 message validation status</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 Sender name</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### AS2 Mdn Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>As2 Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 unique message ID from receiver to sender.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disposition Content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 processed MDN report as headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 Receiver name</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 HTTP headers that elaborate the message format.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn Alg</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MD5</p></li><li><p>SHA1</p></li><li><p>SHA224</p></li><li><p>SHA256</p></li><li><p>SHA384</p></li><li><p>SHA512</p></li><li><p>UNSIGNED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 message integrity check algorithm used</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn Mic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 message integrity check hashcode</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mdn Processed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 message validation status</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Original AS2 Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 Message ID received</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Report Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MDN report text</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 Sender name</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### AS2 Send Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>As2 Mdn Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AS2MdnAttributes">AS2 Mdn Attributes</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 returned MDN attribute group</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>As2 Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 unique message ID from receiver to sender</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 receiver name</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 HTTP headers that elaborate the message format</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Msg MIC</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 message integrity check hashcode</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Msg MIC Alg</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 message integrity check algorithm used</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AS2 sender name</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the value of the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Failure Status Code Validator

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Values</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of status codes that cause errors to be thrown.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### KeyStore

Configures the keystore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### NTLM Proxy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Ntlm Domain</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Domain of the NTLM proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname or IP address of the NTLM proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the NTLM proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Proxy Hosts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NTLM hosts that the connector can access without using a proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname or IP address of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Non Proxy Hosts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hosts that the connector can access without using a proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Attempts</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency (ms)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to reconnect, in milliseconds</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>true: Allow the deployment to fail.</p></li><li><p>false: Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering a process-failed message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is SHA-256.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property can be set only if useSecureHash is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk.</p><div><p>This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></div></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which the value of <strong>In Memory Size</strong> is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the value
of the **MAX Buffer Size** field. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for
your specific use case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Max Buffer Size</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Failback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method, which is the method not specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server cannot be reached or is busy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Success Status Code Validator

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Values</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of status codes that indicate success.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">KeyStore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#crl-file">Crl File</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom Ocsp Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm used by the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this field to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [AS2 Connector in Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.connectors/mule-as2-connector/)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure AS2 Connector 7.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure an input source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure a Global Element for External Libraries](#configure-external-libraries).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[AS2 Connector Reference](as2-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add AS2 Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the connectorâs
namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `as2` in the search field.
3.  Click **AS2** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these input
sources to use with AS2 Connector:

- **As 2 listener**  
  Listens for AS2 `send` requests
- **As 2 mdn listener**  
  MDN listener that listens for AS2 listener requests
- **Non repudiation listener**  
  Listens for signed messages and MDNs that are configured for non-repudiation storage
- **HTTP Listener**  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- **Scheduler**  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an **HTTP Listener**, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP listener in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for AS2 Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **AS2 Connector** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials the connector uses to access the target AS2 system.

To configure the global element for AS2 Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **Authentication** tab, configure the authentication method for the outbound request.

    You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
    (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
    properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
    configure them, see
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

## Configure a Global Element for External Libraries

Mule applications using AS2 Connector must contain only one global element configuration to provide
the external libraries required for the selected security mode.

To configure the global element for AS2 Connector and add external libraries, follow these steps:

1.  In **Package Explorer** or Anypoint Studio canvas, open your Mule flow or configuration.
2.  Click **Global Elements**. Or click **Create global element**, and then click the plus icon next
    to Global Elements.
3.  Click **Create**, and then search for the `AS2` or `External Libs` option and select **AS2
    Connector External Libs - Non FIPS 140-3**.
4.  Name the configuration.
5.  Click **Configure** > **Add recommended libraries**.
6.  Click **OK** to include the libraries as dependencies and shared libraries in the Mule
    application `pom.xml` file.
7.  Verify that your project has the required dependencies for your security mode.

    Add the dependencies via Maven if they arenât added automatically.

For configuration instructions for other security modes, see
[Configure External Libraries](as2-connector-upgrade-migrate#configure-external-libraries).

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## Next Step

After you configure a global element and connection information, configure the other fields for the
connector.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [AS2 Reference](as2-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating AS2 Connector to Version 7.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for AS2 (AS2 Connector) from version 6.x to 7.x to take advantage of
FIPS-compliant cryptographic support and other improvements.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>6.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>7.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Before You Begin

Before you upgrade to AS2 Connector 7.x, make sure you have:

- Mule runtime 4.4.0 or later. See the Mule runtime release notes for FIPS support by version.
- AS2 Connector version 7.0.0 or later.
- AS2 Support Libraries version 3.0.0 or later.
- For non-FIPS mode, Bouncy Castle Java Cryptography Extension (JCE) provider designed for JDK 1.8
  or higher. This configuration requires the `bcprov-jdk18on` and `bcpkix-jdk18on` libraries.
  Version 1.82 is recommended.
- For FIPS modes, Bouncy Castle FIPS (BCFIPS) libraries as required by your Mule runtime and
  security model.
- For FIPS 140-3, Java 17 or later with FIPS-capable JVM if required by your deployment.

## Supported Security Modes

AS2 Connector 7.x supports these security modes, which are controlled by the `mule.security.model`
system property:

- **non-FIPS (default)**

  This is the default security mode for Mule applications that donât require FIPS compliance. AS2
  Connector uses the standard cryptographic libraries `bcprov-jdk18on` and `bcpkix-jdk18on`, which
  arenât FIPS compliant. Donât set the system property as the default mode.

- **FIPS 140-2 Compliant Mode**

  Enable this security mode to configure AS2 Connector to use FIPS 140-2 approved cryptographic
  providers.

- **FIPS 140-3 Compliant Mode**

  Enable this security mode to configure AS2 Connector to use FIPS 140-3 approved cryptographic
  providers.

If the `mule.security.model` property begins with `fips140-`, then AS2 Connector activates FIPS mode
and applies the corresponding security controls, as shown in the example.

```bash
-Dmule.security.model=fips140-3
```

## Configure External Libraries

Mule applications using AS2 Connector require a Global Element configuration that includes the
necessary AS2 support and cryptographic libraries for each security mode. The table shows the global
configuration and required libraries for each security mode. Use only one security mode per
application.

For FIPS modes, the Mule runtime and BCFIPS requirements apply.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Security Mode</th><th>Global Configuration</th><th>Default Name</th><th>Required Library Maven Coordinates</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>non-FIPS</p></td><td><p><code>AS2 Connector External Libs - Non FIPS</code></p></td><td><p><code>AS2_Connector_External_Libs___Non_FIPS</code></p></td><td><p>com.mulesoft.connectors:as2-bc-non-fips-impl:3.0.0, org.bouncycastle:bcpkix-jdk18on:1.82, org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk18on:1.82</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FIPS 140-2</p></td><td><p><code>AS2 Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-2</code></p></td><td><p><code>AS2_Connector_External_Libs___FIPS_140_2</code></p></td><td><p>com.mulesoft.connectors:as2-bc1-fips-impl:3.0.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FIPS 140-3</p></td><td><p><code>AS2 Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-3</code></p></td><td><p><code>AS2_Connector_External_Libs___FIPS_140_3</code></p></td><td><p>com.mulesoft.connectors:as2-bc2-fips-impl:3.0.0</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

![Connector configurations that are used for creating Global Elements](_images/as2-connector-upgrade-global-element-config.png)

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to upgrade from AS2 Connector 6.x to 7.x. Use the configuration that applies to
your selected security mode.

### Step 1: Update the Connector Dependency

1.  Update your projectâs `pom.xml` file to use AS2 Connector 7.x:

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
    <artifactId>mule-as2-connector</artifactId>
    <version>7.0.0</version>
    <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

2.  Rebuild the project and resolve API or configuration changes reported by the build.

### Step 2: Add a Connector Configuration Global Element

Include only one external library global configuration. Follow the global element configuration
steps for your selected security mode.

#### Configure a Global Element for the non-FIPS Security Mode

1.  Add the external library global configuration and donât set the runtime property. Also, make
    sure the BouncyCastle jdk18on library version 1.82 is available to add as an external library.
2.  Configure the global element as a Mule app in Anypoint Studio:
    1.  In the **Package Explorer** or Anypoint Studio canvas, open your Mule flow or configuration.
    2.  Click **Global Elements**. Or click **Create global element**, and then click the plus icon
        next to Global Elements.

3.  Click **Create**, and then search for the `AS2` or `External Libs` option and select **AS2
    Connector External Libs - Non FIPS**.
    1.  Name the configuration.
    2.  Click **Configure** > **Add recommended libraries**.
    3.  Click **OK** to include the libraries as dependencies and shared libraries in the Mule
        application `pom.xml` file.

4.  Verify that your project has the required dependencies for your selected security mode. Add the
    dependencies via Maven if they arenât added automatically.

![The Connector Configuration Global Element and the configurations for non-FIPS security mode](_images/as2-connector-upgrade-non-fips-config.png)

To configure the global element manually in XML (Maven or standalone):

1.  Open your main Mule configuration file, such as `src/main/mule/` or `src/main/resources/`.
2.  Add the `as2-mule4:external-libs-non-fips-config` global element to the Mule configuration file.
3.  Add the `AS2 Connector External Libs - Non FIPS` global configuration to your Mule configuration
    file, as shown in the example:

```xml
<as2-mule4:external-libs-non-fips-config name="AS2_External_Libs_Non_FIPS" />
```

4.  Add the required Maven dependencies to the `pom.xml` file. Refer to the
    [AS2 Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/as2-connector-release-notes-mule-4)
    for the compatible versions of the dependencies.

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
    <artifactId>bcpkix-jdk18on</artifactId>
    <version>${bouncy.castle.jdk18on.version}</version>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
    <artifactId>bcprov-jdk18on</artifactId>
    <version>${bouncy.castle.jdk18on.version}</version>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
    <artifactId>as2-bc-non-fips-impl</artifactId>
    <version>${as2.support.library.version}</version>
</dependency>
```

5.  Update the `mule-maven-plugin` by including the shared libraries in the **build** >
    **plugins** > **plugin** section of the `pom.xml` file.

```xml
<configuration>
  <sharedLibraries>
    <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
      <artifactId>as2-bc-non-fips-impl</artifactId>
    </sharedLibrary>
    <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
      <artifactId>bcpkix-jdk18on</artifactId>
    </sharedLibrary>
    <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
      <artifactId>bcprov-jdk18on</artifactId>
    </sharedLibrary>
  </sharedLibraries>
</configuration>
```

#### Configure a Global Element for the FIPS 140-2 Security Mode

1.  Add the external library global configuration and set the runtime property. Also, make sure
    BouncyCastle FIPS (BCFIPS) is available as required by your Mule runtime. Configure BCFIPS in
    Java Security.

    For FIPS 140-2, follow the
    [FIPS 140-2 Compliance Support](../../mule-runtime/latest/fips-140-2-compliance-support)
    documentation.

2.  Configure the global element as a Mule app in Anypoint Studio:
    1.  In the **Package Explorer** or Anypoint Studio canvas, open your Mule flow or configuration.
    2.  Click **Global Elements**. Or click **Create global element**, and then click the plus icon
        next to Global Elements.

3.  Click **Create**, and then search for the `AS2` or `External Libs` option and select **AS2
    Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-2**.
    1.  Name the configuration.
    2.  Click **Configure** > **Add recommended libraries**.
    3.  Click **OK** to include the libraries as dependencies and shared libraries in the Mule
        application `pom.xml` file.

4.  Verify that your project has the required dependencies for your selected security mode. Add the
    dependencies via Maven if they arenât added automatically.

![The Connector Configuration Global Element and the configurations for FIPS 140-2 security mode](_images/as2-connector-upgrade-fips140-2-config.png)

To configure the global element manually in XML (Maven or standalone):

1.  Open your main Mule configuration file, such as `src/main/mule/` or `src/main/resources/`.
2.  Add the `as2-mule4:external-libs-fips140-2-config` global element to the Mule configuration
    file.
3.  Add the `AS2 Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-2` global configuration to your Mule
    configuration file, as shown in the example.

```xml
<as2-mule4:external-libs-fips140-2-config name="AS2_External_Libs_FIPS_140_2" />
```

4.  Add the required Maven dependencies to the `pom.xml` file. Refer to the
    [AS2 Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/as2-connector-release-notes-mule-4)
    for the compatible versions of the dependencies.

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
    <artifactId>as2-bc1-fips-impl</artifactId>
    <version>${as2.support.library.version}</version>
</dependency>
```

5.  Update the `mule-maven-plugin` by including the shared libraries in the **build** >
    **plugins** > **plugin** section of the `pom.xml` file.

```xml
<configuration>
  <sharedLibraries>
    <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
      <artifactId>as2-bc1-fips-impl</artifactId>
    </sharedLibrary>
  </sharedLibraries>
</configuration>
```

6.  In JVM or Mule runtime, set the security model to FIPS 140-2.

```bash
-Dmule.security.model=fips140-2
```

7.  Restrict BCFIPS to use only FIPS-approved algorithms.

```bash
-Dorg.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true
```

This example shows the Mule runtime startup configuration for FIPS 140-2:

```bash
java -Dmule.security.model=fips140-2 \
  -Dorg.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true \
  -jar mule-standalone.jar
```

#### Configure a Global Element for the FIPS 140-3 Security Mode

1.  Add the external library global configuration and set the runtime property. Also:
    - Make sure BouncyCastle FIPS (BCFIPS) is available as required by your Mule runtime.
    - Configure BCFIPS in Java Security or by following the Mule runtime FIPS documentation for your
      version.
    - Make sure you have Java 17 or later as required for FIPS 140-3 support in your deployment.

2.  Configure the global element as a Mule app in Anypoint Studio:
    1.  In the **Package Explorer** or Anypoint Studio canvas, open your Mule flow or configuration.
    2.  Click **Global Elements**. Or click **Create global element**, and then click the plus icon
        next to Global Elements.

3.  Click **Create**, and then search for the `AS2` or `External Libs` option and select **AS2
    Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-3**.
    1.  Name the configuration.
    2.  Click **Configure** > **Add recommended libraries**.
    3.  Click **OK** to include the libraries as dependencies and shared libraries in the Mule
        application `pom.xml` file.

4.  Verify that your project has the required dependencies for your selected security mode. Add the
    dependencies via Maven if they arenât added automatically.

![The Connector Configuration Global Element and the configurations for FIPS 140-3 security mode](_images/as2-connector-upgrade-fips140-3-config.png)

To configure the global element manually in XML (Maven or standalone):

1.  Open your main Mule configuration file, such as `src/main/mule/` or `src/main/resources/`.
2.  Add the `as2-mule4:external-libs-fips140-3-config` global element to the Mule configuration
    file.
3.  Add the `AS2 Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-3` global configuration to your Mule
    configuration file, as shown in the example:

```xml
<as2-mule4:external-libs-fips140-3-config name="AS2_External_Libs_FIPS_140_3" />
```

4.  Add the required Maven dependencies to the `pom.xml` file. Refer to the
    [AS2 Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/as2-connector-release-notes-mule-4)
    for the compatible versions of the dependencies.

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
    <artifactId>as2-bc2-fips-impl</artifactId>
    <version>${as2.support.library.version}</version>
</dependency>
```

5.  Update the `mule-maven-plugin` by including the shared libraries in the **build** >
    **plugins** > **plugin** section of the `pom.xml` file.

```xml
<configuration>
  <sharedLibraries>
    <sharedLibrary>
      <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
      <artifactId>as2-bc2-fips-impl</artifactId>
    </sharedLibrary>
  </sharedLibraries>
</configuration>
```

6.  In JVM or Mule runtime, set the security model to FIPS 140-3.

```bash
-Dmule.security.model=fips140-3
```

7.  Restrict BCFIPS to use only FIPS-approved algorithms.

```bash
-Dorg.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true
```

This example shows the Mule runtime startup configuration for FIPS 140-3:

```bash
java -Dmule.security.model=fips140-3 \
  -Dorg.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true \
  -jar mule-standalone.jar
```

> [!NOTE] The `org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only` property is JVM-wide and must be set in your
> system or runtime configuration when running in FIPS mode.
>
> If you use BCFIPS in FIPS mode, key and algorithm restrictions apply. Only approved algorithms
> supported by the BCFIPS provider are allowed.

### Step 3: Verify Keystore and Algorithm Compatibility

1.  Verify that keystores and certificates are compatible with your selected security mode. Some
    FIPS modes require FIPS-approved algorithms.
2.  Review the Mule and BCFIPS documentation for approved algorithms if you use BCFIPS in FIPS mode,
    since key and algorithm restrictions apply.

### Step 4: Verify Connector Usage in Flows

1.  Verify that partner certificates and algorithms are compatible with your selected security mode.
2.  If you use AS2 Connector operations in your flows, verify that the connector configuration also
    exists in your Mule app.

## AS2 Connector Startup Validation

When an AS2 listener, AS2 MDN listener, or Send operation starts, AS2 Connector validates that the
external library configuration and runtime settings are correct. Validation fails if the Mule
application includes listener, MDN listener, or Send operations that donât meet these requirements:

- The application must include one external library. An error occurs if more than one external
  library is included or if none are included.
- The `mule.security.model` system property must match the selected external library configuration:
  - For non-FIPS, `mule.security.model` must be disabled. If itâs enabled, remove the property.
  - For FIPS 140-2, `mule.security.model` must be set to `fips140-2`. If the property is configured
    incorrectly, a message is logged to correct the setting to `-Dmule.security.model=fips140-2`.
  - For FIPS 140-3, `mule.security.model` must be set to `fips140-3`. If the property is configured
    incorrectly, a message is logged to correct the setting to `-Dmule.security.model=fips140-3`.

- The implementation library for the selected security mode must be on the same `classpath`. If the
  library is missing, startup fails. To resolve the issue, review the error log to find the
  conflicting library and remove it from the `classpath`.

  Use the implementation library that applies to your security mode:
  - non-FIPS: `as2-bc-non-fips-impl`
  - FIPS 140-2: `as2-bc1-fips-impl`
  - FIPS 140-3: `as2-bc2-fips-impl`

- AS2 Connector instantiates the cryptographic implementation for the selected security mode at
  startup. If this fails, for example due to missing or incompatible libraries, an error is logged
  that includes the security profile and the cause.

## Post-Upgrade Steps

1.  Verify that the application starts without errors and that AS2 Connector initializes correctly
    for your selected security mode.
2.  Test the AS2 listener, MDN listener, and Send operations against your AS2 partners.
3.  Confirm that certificates and algorithms are accepted in FIPS mode if applicable.
4.  Notify partners of URL or port changes if you modified the HTTP Listener configuration.

## System Properties Reference

The table details the system-level properties that are required to configure AS2 Connectorâs
security modes and BCFIPS compliance within the Mule runtime or JVM.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th><th>Values</th><th>Default</th><th>Scope</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>mule.security.model</code></p></td><td><p>Security mode used by the connector</p></td><td><p><code>fips140-2</code> or <code>fips140-3</code></p></td><td><p><code>non-FIPS</code> (when not set)</p></td><td><p>Mule runtime or JVM</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only</code></p></td><td><p>Restricts BCFIPS to FIPS-approved algorithms only. Required for FIPS compliance when using BCFIPS.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code>, <code>false</code></p></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td><p>JVM-wide</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

The `org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only` property affects the entire JVM. When the application runs
in FIPS mode, the system administrator must configure the property. For more information, see the
**FIPS 140-2 Compliance Support** section on the [Security](../../mule-runtime/latest/securing) page
in the Mule runtime documentation.

## Migrate Existing Deployments

AS2 Connector 7.x introduces a Global Connector Configuration and supports configurable security
modes including non-FIPS, FIPS 140-2, and FIPS 140-3 via the `mule.security.model` system property.
Follow the [Upgrade Steps](#upgrade-steps) to make the necessary changes to your Mule app. AS2
Connectorâs cryptographic behavior is determined by the global configuration, which provides the
required libraries and defines the runtime security model.

Existing 6.x flows remain functional without requiring code changes.

### Migrate Deployments from FIPS 140-2 or 140-3 Environments

1.  Verify that BCFIPS and FIPS-related settings are configured correctly for your Mule runtime
    version. For more information, see the **FIPS 140-2 Compliance Support** section on the
    [Security](../../mule-runtime/latest/securing) page in the Mule runtime documentation.
2.  Set `mule.security.model` to `fips140-2` or `fips140-3` as appropriate for your compliance
    requirements.
3.  Set `org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only` to `true` if required by your runtime for compliance.
4.  Test your configuration in a non-production environment by running your flows with the AS2
    listener, MDN listener, and Send operations.
5.  Deploy the application to production and monitor the error log to verify a successful startup
    and AS2 message exchange.

## Troubleshooting

These common error messages and issues indicate configuration or `classpath` problems that prevent
AS2 Connector from operating in the selected security mode. Follow the recommendations to resolve
the issues.

- **AS2 Connector requires at least one External Libs configuration**

  Add `AS2 Connector External Libs - Non FIPS`, `AS2 Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-2`, or
  `AS2 Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-3` to your application. See
  [AS2 Connector Startup Validation](#startup-validation).

- **Only one External Libs configuration is allowed per application**

  Remove the additional external library configurations so that you only have one.

- **Detected 'as2-bc-non-fips-impl' but system property 'mule.security.model' is â¦â**

  For non-FIPS mode, disable `mule.security.model` or remove it from the JVM arguments or runtime
  configuration.

- **Detected 'as2-bc1-fips-impl' but system property â¦â Set -Dmule.security.model=fips140-2**

  Add `-Dmule.security.model=fips140-2` if youâre using `AS2 Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-2`.

- **Detected 'as2-bc2-fips-impl' but system property â¦â Set -Dmule.security.model=fips140-3**

  Add `-Dmule.security.model=fips140-3` if youâre using `AS2 Connector External Libs - FIPS 140-3`.

- **External Libs - â¦â configuration selected, but 'â¦â' is not on the classpath**

  Add the correct implementation dependency for your selected security mode or make sure the
  external library configuration imports the dependency. If you receive a warning, make sure only
  the library for your active security mode remains on the `classpath` by removing any additional
  AS2 cryptographic implementations.

- **Startup or Crypto Errors in FIPS Mode**

  Make sure `mule.security.model` matches your runtime FIPS setup and that BCFIPS is installed and
  registered. Set `org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only` to `true` if required.

- **Tests or Flows that Relied on non-FIPS Behavior**

  If your application is running with `mule.security.model=fips140-2` or `fips140-3`, some
  algorithms or key usages are restricted. Align keystores and partner expectations with
  FIPS-approved algorithms.

- **Caching or Metadata Issues**

  If Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Design Center shows a stale configuration, try restarting the IDE
  or clearing your cache.

## Revert the Upgrade

To revert to AS2 Connector 6.x:

1.  Change the `mule-as2-connector` dependency in your projectâs `pom.xml` file to the desired 6.x
    version.
2.  Remove the external library global configuration.
3.  Remove the libraries that were added as dependencies and shared libraries from the `pom.xml`
    file.
4.  Remove or adjust 7.x system properties, such as `mule.security.model`, if they arenât being used
    by other components.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [AS2 Connector Reference](as2-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# AS2 Connector 7.0 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio.

If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML
editor or from a text editor, you can get access to the connector in your app by adding reference
statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

When you install the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location. It also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2/current/mule-as2-mule4.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
<[source,xml,linenums]
<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
xmlns:as2="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/as2-mule4"
xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:spring="http://www.springframework.org/schema/beans"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	    xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.springframework.org/schema/beans
```
